name: Dialogue Choice
description: Dialogue Choice is an interface mechanic that presents game state, accepts decisions, or reduces player friction.
design_notes: Dialogue Choice should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For ui ux mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use dialogue choice as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect dialogue choice to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels informed without fighting the interface.

parameters.name: display_duration
parameters.description: How long the interface element remains visible after triggering.

parameters.name: priority_level
parameters.description: Layering or importance compared with other UI messages.

parameters.name: input_focus_mode
parameters.description: Control mode the UI must support cleanly.

edge_cases: Dialogue Choice is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Dialogue Choice overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Dialogue Choice runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Dialogue Choice state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Dialogue Choice plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Dialogue Choice produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Dialogue Choice strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Dialogue Choice becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Dialogue Choice with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Dialogue Choice as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Dialogue Choice with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Dialogue Choice as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
